channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Artemis II Astronauts Discuss Lunar Mission and Experiences at Ottawa Event
item.description: The crew of NASA's Artemis II mission recently participated in a public event at Ottawa's National Arts Centre, where they answered audience questions and shared their unique experiences from the miss...

item.title: Chloe Fineman's Relationship Status and Personal Life Explained
item.description: Chloe Fineman, the American actress and comedian, is not married, contrary to rumours caused by her 2020 fake Instagram wedding with actor Casey Thomas Brown. The two met through mutual work contacts ...

item.title: Behind the Scenes of the 1986 Film 'About Last Night...': Cast, Locations, and Legacy
item.description: Released in 1986, 'About Last Night...' is a notable romantic comedy-drama from the Brat Pack era that explores the complexities of relationships among twenty-somethings in Chicago. Directed by Edward...

item.title: Covid-19 Pandemic Caused Significant Excess Mortality Worldwide Between 2020-2023
item.description: The World Health Statistics report has highlighted a stark reality: between 2020 and 2023, the Covid-19 pandemic caused an estimated 22.1 million excess deaths globally, which is more than three times...

item.title: Botswana Mourns Former President Festus Mogae at Age 86
item.description: Former Botswana president, Festus Mogae, don pass away at 86, as dem announce am for national address. President Duma Boko talk say Mogae die after him dey battle some health wahala. The government do...

item.title: Women Leaders Take Key Roles in Organizing Karate One-Youth League Manila
item.description: The upcoming Karate One-Youth League event in Manila is set to feature significant contributions from three prominent women who will play crucial roles in organizing the competition. These women are r...

item.title: A Look at How Television Shaped Mid-20th Century Life
item.description: During the mid-20th century, television transformed from a luxury item into a central part of home life. By the 1950s, often referred to as the 'Golden Age of Television,' families were gathering arou...

item.title: Understanding Andes Virus: Why It Is Unlikely to Cause a Pandemic
item.description: News of a hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship has understandably caused concern, as memories of the Covid-19 pandemic remain fresh. The specific virus involved, Andes virus, is indeed se...

item.title: Analysis of the Federal Budget Highlights Spending Concerns Amid Positive Measures
item.description: The recently announced federal budget has drawn a mix of praise and caution. While it includes measures aimed at addressing long-standing issues identified by Treasury and the Productivity Commission,...

item.title: Alberta court halts petition for provincial separation due to First Nations concerns
item.description: An Alberta Court of King’s Bench judge has overturned Elections Alberta’s approval of a petition that sought to trigger a referendum on Alberta separating from Canada. Justice Sheila Leonard ruled tha...

item.description: In Meru County, residents teamed up with Principal Secretary for East African Community Affairs, Dr. Caroline Karugu, and staff from the State Department of EAC Affairs in a tree planting drive suppor...

item.title: NASA Develops Nuclear Propulsion to Shorten Mars Travel Time
item.description: NASA is advancing the use of nuclear propulsion to dramatically reduce travel times to Mars, potentially cutting journeys from over six months to just three or four. This initiative has gained momentu...

item.title: Gauteng traffic officer granted bail pending investigation for alleged Arcadia sexual assault
item.description: A 36-year-old Gauteng traffic officer has been granted bail of R3,000 following allegations that he raped a 26-year-old woman in an Arcadia apartment building. According to reports from the National P...

item.title: Senator Dela Rosa remains in office as legal challenges continue at Supreme Court
item.description: Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa will continue serving his term in the Philippine Senate despite ongoing legal disputes. Lawyers involved in the case have indicated plans to bring the matter back to th...

item.title: Tamil Nadu CM urges end of NEET after exam paper leak, suggests Class 12 marks for medical admissions
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay has called for the immediate abolition of the NEET-UG exam following a major paper leak incident. The National Testing Agency (NTA) had to cancel the 2026 NEE...

item.title: Emily Atack and Cast Shine at Rivals Series Two Premiere in London
item.description: Emily Atack led the arrivals at the Rivals series two premiere at the BFI IMAX on South Bank, London. The 36-year-old actress, portraying the provocative Sarah Stratton, stunned in a plunging silver g...

item.title: Johannesburg collects nearly R100m after tackling illegal billboards
item.description: The City of Johannesburg has reported a significant increase in revenue, collecting close to R100 million within a few months following a strengthened crackdown on illegal outdoor advertising. The ini...

item.title: OpenAI CEO Altman Discusses AI Safety Concerns with Elon Musk in Testimony
item.description: Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI, recently provided testimony regarding a discussion he had with Elon Musk about AI safety. The conversation reportedly focused on the potential risks associated with advanced...

item.title: Portraits and Legacy of Chelo Alonso, Icon of 1950s–60s Italian Cinema
item.description: Chelo Alonso, born Isabel Apolonia García Hernández in 1933, was a Cuban-born dancer and actress who rose to fame in the mid-20th century. Known for her magnetic stage presence and extraordinary beaut...

item.title: Odisha Chief Minister reduces convoy size to promote fuel conservation
item.description: In response to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's recent call for judicious use of petrol amid the ongoing oil crisis triggered by tensions in West Asia, Odisha Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi has taken...
